<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">29</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;"><b><a id="notes" name="notes"></a>APPLIED <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">MINERALS, INC.</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;"><b>(An Exploration Stage Mining Company)</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12693" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980338338" id="thunderdome-statement-858293">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591578" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; ORGANIZATION AND DESCRIPTION OF BUSINESS</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Applied Minerals, Inc. (the &ldquo;Company&rdquo;) is the owner of the Dragon Mine located in the Tintic Mining District of the State of Utah from where it produces halloysite clay and iron oxide. The Company is currently selling its DRAGONITE halloysite clay product regularly to <em data-td-fact-id="29591579" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> (<em data-td-fact-id="29591580" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4</em>) customers. &nbsp;Several prospective customers are conducting either commercial-scale trials or field trials for an array of products that are expected to use DRAGONITE as a functional additive.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November, 2015, </em>the Company entered into an agreement to supply a customer its AMIRON iron oxide product, on an exclusive basis, for a period of <em data-td-fact-id="29591581" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> years. The exclusivity provision is limited to the specialized catalyst application of the Customer and enables Applied Minerals to sell its iron oxide products for use in other technical applications that are <em data-td-fact-id="29591582" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> competitive with the Customer&#39;s intended field of use. An initial purchase order of <em data-td-fact-id="29591583" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5.0</em> million of AMIRON products was obtained in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November, 2015. </em>By <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June, 2017, </em>the Company had fulfilled the order. Upon expiration of the initial <em data-td-fact-id="29591584" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em>-year term, the customer has an option to extend the exclusive supply agreement for an additional <em data-td-fact-id="29591585" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em> years by issuing an <em data-td-fact-id="29591586" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8.0</em> million purchase order to be delivered over the course of the subsequent <em data-td-fact-id="29591587" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twenty-four</em> (<em data-td-fact-id="29591588" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">24</em>) months. There is the possibility this customer <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>order additional AMIRON before the initial term of the agreement expires.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Applied Minerals, Inc. is a publicly traded company incorporated in the state of Delaware. The common stock trades on the OTC Bulletin Board under the symbol &ldquo;AMNL.&rdquo;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">For the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="29591589" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> revenues from the Company&rsquo;s largest customer accounted for <em data-td-fact-id="29591590" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">56%</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591591" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">88%</em> of total revenues, respectively. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="29591592" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> amounts owed from this customer comprised <em data-td-fact-id="29591593" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0%</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591594" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">90%</em> of accounts receivable, respectively.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12694" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980348962" id="thunderdome-statement-858294">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591595" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em> - LIQUIDITY&nbsp;AND BASIS OF PRESENTATION</b></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The Company has a history of recurring losses from operations and the use of cash in operating activities. For the <em data-td-fact-id="29591596" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em> months ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company&rsquo;s net loss was <em data-td-fact-id="29591597" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$14,910,659</em> and cash used in operating activities was <em data-td-fact-id="29591598" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2,108,388.</em> As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company had current assets of <em data-td-fact-id="29591599" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$280,839</em> and current liabilities of <em data-td-fact-id="29591600" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,233,077</em> of which <em data-td-fact-id="29591601" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$57,334</em> was accrued PIK Note interest to be paid in additional PIK Notes. The Company&rsquo;s current liabilities also include approximately (i) <em data-td-fact-id="29591602" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$204,700</em> of accrued management bonus payments, which management <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>choose to take in the form of shares of common stock or options to purchase shares of common depending on the Company&rsquo;s ability to pay the bonuses in cash as determined by the Company&rsquo;s Audit Committee, (ii) <em data-td-fact-id="29591603" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$158,700</em> of payables to a compounder for which it has agreed to satisfy in halloysite product, (iii) <em data-td-fact-id="29591604" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$156,200</em> of disputed accrued expenses for which the Company believes it has a statute of limitations defense, and (iv) <em data-td-fact-id="29591605" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$15,000</em> of director expense accrual that will be paid in equity.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Management believes that in order for the Company to meet its obligations arising from normal business operations through <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 17, 2019 </em>that the Company requires (i) additional capital either in the form of a private placement of common stock or debt and/or (ii) additional sales of its products that will generate sufficient operating profit and cash flows to fund operations. &nbsp;Without additional capital or additional sales of its products, the Company&rsquo;s ability to continue to operate will be limited.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Based on the Company&rsquo;s current cash usage expectations, management believes it will <em data-td-fact-id="29591606" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have sufficient liquidity to fund its operations through <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 17, 2019. </em>Further, management cannot provide any assurance that it is probable that the Company will be successful in accomplishing any of its plans to raise debt or equity financing or generate additional product sales. Collectively these factors raise substantial doubt regarding the Company&rsquo;s ability to continue as going concern. These financial statements do <em data-td-fact-id="29591607" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> include any adjustments to the recoverability and classification of recorded assets amounts and classification of liabilities that might be necessary should the Company <em data-td-fact-id="29591608" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be able to continue as a going concern.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12695" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980365324" id="thunderdome-statement-858295">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591609" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; SUMMARY OF SIGNIFICANT ACCOUNTING POLICIES</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Exploration-Stage Company</u></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Effective <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2009, </em>the Company was, and still is, classified as an exploration company because the existence of proven or probable reserves at the Company&rsquo;s Dragon Mine property have <em data-td-fact-id="29591610" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> been demonstrated and <em data-td-fact-id="29591611" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> significant revenue has been earned from the mine. Under the SEC&rsquo;s Industry Guide <em data-td-fact-id="29591612" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">7,</em> a mining company is considered an exploration stage company until it has declared mineral reserves determined in accordance with the guide and staff interpretations thereof.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Principles of Consolidation</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The accompanying consolidated financial statements include the accounts of Applied Minerals, Inc. and its inactive subsidiary, which holds <em data-td-fact-id="29591613" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">100</em> acres of timber and mineral property in northern Idaho.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Use of Estimates</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The preparation of financial statements in conformity with accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America (&ldquo;GAAP&rdquo;) requires management to make estimates and assumptions that affect reported amounts of assets and liabilities, disclosure of contingent assets and liabilities at the date of the financial statements and reported amounts of revenues and expenses during the reporting period. In these consolidated financial statements, the warrant and PIK note derivative liabilities, stock compensation, impairment of long-lived assets and valuation allowance on income taxes involve extensive reliance on management<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s estimates. Actual results could differ from those estimates.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">30</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Cash and Cash Equivalents</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Cash and cash equivalents include all highly liquid investments with a maturity of <em data-td-fact-id="29591614" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> months or less. The Company minimizes its credit risk by investing its cash and cash equivalents, which sometimes exceeds FDIC limits, with major financial institutions located in the United States with a high credit rating.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Receivables</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Trade receivables are reported at outstanding principal amounts, net of an allowance for doubtful accounts.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Management evaluates the collectability of receivable account balances to determine the allowance, if any. Management considers the other party<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s credit risk and financial condition, as well as current and projected economic and market conditions, in determining the amount of the allowance. Receivable balances are written off when management determines that the balance is uncollectable. <em data-td-fact-id="29591615" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No</em> allowance was required at <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="29591616" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Property and Equipment</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Property and equipment are carried at cost net of accumulated depreciation and amortization. Depreciation and amortization is computed on the straight-line method over the estimated useful lives of the assets, or the life of the lease, whichever is shorter, as follows:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Depreciation expense for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, </em><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="29591617" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591618" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> totaled <em data-td-fact-id="29591619" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,316,537,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591620" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,348,860,</em> respectively.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Impairment of Long-lived Assets</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The Company periodically reviews the carrying amounts of long-lived assets to determine whether current events or circumstances warrant adjustment to such carrying amounts. Long-lived assets are tested for recoverability whenever events or changes in circumstances indicate that its carrying amount <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em><em data-td-fact-id="29591621" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be recoverable. When such events occur, the Company compares the sum of the undiscounted cash flows expected to result from the use and eventual disposition of the asset to its carrying amount. If this comparison indicates that there is an impairment, the amount of the impairment is typically calculated using discounted expected future cash flows where observable fair values are <em data-td-fact-id="29591622" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> readily determinable. Considerable management judgment is necessary to estimate the fair value of assets. Assets to be disposed of are carried at the lower of their financial statement carrying amount or fair value, less cost to sell. The Company has determined that there was <em data-td-fact-id="29591623" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> impairment of its long-lived assets as of December <em data-td-fact-id="29591624" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">31,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591625" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591626" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Revenue Recognition</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Revenue includes sales of halloysite clay and iron oxide, and is recognized when title passes to the buyer and when collectability is reasonably assured. Title passes to the buyer based on terms of the sales contract. Product pricing is determined based on contractual arrangements with the Company<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s customers.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Mining Exploration and Development Costs</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Land and mining property are carried at cost. The Company expenses prospecting and mining exploration costs. At the point when a property is determined to have proven and probable reserves, subsequent development costs will be capitalized and will be charged to operations using the units-of-production method over proven and probable reserves. Upon abandonment or sale of a mineral property, all capitalized costs relating to the specific property are written off in the period abandoned or sold and a gain or loss is recognized.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Income taxes</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The Company uses an asset and liability approach which results in the recognition of deferred tax liabilities and assets for the expected future tax consequences or benefits of temporary differences between the financial reporting basis and the tax basis of assets and liabilities, as well as operating loss and tax credit carry forwards, using enacted tax rates in effect in the years in which the differences are expected to reverse.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">In assessing the realizability of deferred tax assets, management considers whether it is more likely than <em data-td-fact-id="29591627" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> that some portion or all of its deferred tax assets will <em data-td-fact-id="29591628" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be realized. Management considers the scheduled reversal of deferred tax liabilities, projected future taxable income and tax planning strategies in making this assessment. A full valuation allowance has been provided for the Company<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s net deferred tax assets as it is more likely than <em data-td-fact-id="29591629" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> that they will <em data-td-fact-id="29591630" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be realized.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Authoritative guidance provides that the tax effects from an uncertain tax position taken or expected to be taken in a tax return can be recognized in our financial statements only if the position is more likely than <em data-td-fact-id="29591631" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> of being sustained on audit based on the technical merits of the position. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, </em><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="29591632" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591633" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> benefit from uncertain tax positions was recognized in our financial statements. The Company has elected to classify interest and/or penalties related to income tax matters in income tax expense.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">31</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Stock Options and Warrants</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The Company follows ASC <em data-td-fact-id="29591634" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">718</em> (Stock Compensation) and <em data-td-fact-id="29591635" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">505</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="29591636" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50</em> (Equity-Based Payments to Non-employees), which provide guidance in accounting for share-based awards exchanged for services rendered and requires companies to expense the estimated fair value of these awards over the requisite service period. The Company instituted a formal long-term and short-term incentive plan on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 20, 2012, </em>which was approved by its shareholders. Prior to that date, we did <em data-td-fact-id="29591637" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have a formal equity plan, but all equity grants, including stock options and warrants, were approved by our Board of Directors. We determine the fair value of the stock-based compensation awards granted to non-employees as either the fair value of the consideration received or the fair value of the equity instruments issued, whichever is more reliably measurable. If the fair value of the equity instruments issued is used, it is measured using the stock price and other measurement assumptions as of the earlier of either of (<em data-td-fact-id="29591638" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>) the date at which a commitment for performance by the counterparty to earn the equity instruments is reached, or (<em data-td-fact-id="29591639" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em>) the date at which the counterparty<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s performance is complete. Beginning in the quarter ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June 30, 2013 </em>the Company began using the simplified method to determine the expected term for any options granted because the Company did <em data-td-fact-id="29591640" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have sufficient historical exercise data to provide a reasonable basis upon which to estimate expected term. The Company previously utilized the contractual term as the expected term.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Environmental Matters</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Expenditures for ongoing compliance with environmental regulations that relate to current operations are expensed or capitalized as appropriate. Expenditures resulting from the remediation of existing conditions caused by past operations that do <em data-td-fact-id="29591641" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> contribute to future revenue generations are expensed. Liabilities are recognized when environmental assessments indicate that remediation efforts are probable and the costs can be reasonably estimated.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Estimates of such liabilities are based upon currently available facts, existing technology and presently enacted laws and regulations taking into consideration the likely effects of inflation and other societal and economic factors, and include estimates of associated legal costs. These amounts also reflect prior experience in remediating contaminated sites, other companies<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo; clean-up experience and data released by The Environmental Protection Agency or other organizations. Such estimates are by their nature imprecise and can be expected to be revised over time because of changes in government regulations, operations, technology and inflation. Recoveries are evaluated separately from the liability and, when recovery is assured, the Company records and reports an asset separately from the associated liability.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">Based upon management<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s current assessment of its environmental responsibilities, it does <em data-td-fact-id="29591642" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> believe that any reclamation or remediation liability exists at <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:27pt;margin-right:0pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;text-indent:-27pt;"><b>Recent </b><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b>I</b><b>ssued </b><b>Accounting Pronouncements</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> May 2014, </em>the Financial Accounting Standards Board (&ldquo;FASB&rdquo;) issued guidance on revenue recognition, which provides a single, comprehensive revenue recognition model for all contracts with customers and supersedes most existing revenue recognition guidance.&nbsp; The main principle under this guidance is that an entity should recognize revenue at the amount it expects to be entitled to in exchange for the transfer of goods or services to customers.&nbsp; The Company has identified the predominant changes to its accounting policies resulting from the application of this guidance and is in the process of quantifying the impact on its consolidated financial statements.&nbsp; The cumulative effect of the initial adoption will be reflected as an adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings as of the date of the application of the guidance; however, this guidance will <em data-td-fact-id="29591643" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have a significant impact on the Company&rsquo;s consolidated financial statements as the Company does <em data-td-fact-id="29591644" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have significant customer contracts in place at <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017.&nbsp; </em>The Company will adopt this guidance <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018 </em>as required.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016, </em>the FASB issued ASU <em data-td-fact-id="29591645" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="29591646" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">02</em> (&ldquo;Topic <em data-td-fact-id="29591647" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">842&rdquo;</em>) new accounting guidance for leases, which supersedes previous lease guidance. Under this guidance, for all leases with terms in excess of <em data-td-fact-id="29591648" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> year, including operating leases, the Company will be required to recognize on its balance sheet a lease liability and a right-of-use asset representing its right to use the underlying asset for the lease term. The new guidance retains a distinction between finance leases and operating leases and the classification criteria is substantially similar to previous guidance. Additionally, the recognition, measurement, and presentation of expenses and cash flows arising from a lease by a lessee have <em data-td-fact-id="29591649" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> significantly changed. The Company is currently evaluating the impact is guidance on its consolidated balance sheets. This guidance is effective for interim and annual reporting periods beginning after <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2018, </em>with early adoption permitted.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12696" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12697" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980406211" id="thunderdome-statement-858299">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591651" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; ASSETS HELD FOR SALE</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 2015, </em>the Company put <em data-td-fact-id="29591652" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> parcels owned in Idaho up for auction. Three of the <em data-td-fact-id="29591653" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> properties sold but settled in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 2016 </em>for a gross sales price of <em data-td-fact-id="29591654" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$172,948.</em> The Company received net process of <em data-td-fact-id="29591655" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$155,187,</em> net of <em data-td-fact-id="29591656" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$17,761</em> of selling expenses. On <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 21, 2016, </em>the Company sold the <em data-td-fact-id="29591657" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> parcel for gross proceeds of <em data-td-fact-id="29591658" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$418,000.</em> The Company received net process of <em data-td-fact-id="29591659" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$380,000,</em> net of a <em data-td-fact-id="29591660" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10%</em> buyer&rsquo;s premium paid to the auction firm, J.P. King. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12698" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980424127" id="thunderdome-statement-858380">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591661" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">6</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; FAIR VALUE MEASUREMENTS AND FINANCIAL INSTRUMENTS</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">ASC Topic <em data-td-fact-id="29591662" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">820,</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i>Fair Value Measurement and Disclosures</i>, defines fair value as the exchange price that would be received for an asset or paid to transfer a liability (an exit price) in the principal or most advantageous market for the asset or liability in an orderly transaction between market participants on the measurement date. This topic also establishes a fair value hierarchy, which requires classification based on observable and unobservable inputs when measuring fair value. The fair value hierarchy distinguishes between assumptions based on market data (observable inputs) and an entity&rsquo;s own assumptions (unobservable inputs). The hierarchy consists of <em data-td-fact-id="29591663" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> levels:</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:3.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Level <em data-td-fact-id="29591664" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; Quoted prices in active markets for identical assets and liabilities;</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:3.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Level <em data-td-fact-id="29591665" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; Inputs other than level <em data-td-fact-id="29591666" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> inputs that are either directly or indirectly observable; and </font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:3.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Level <em data-td-fact-id="29591667" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; Unobservable inputs developed using estimates and assumptions, which are developed by the reporting entity and reflect those assumptions that a market participant would use.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">33</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Liabilities measured at fair value on a recurring basis are summarized as follows:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The recorded value of certain financial assets and liabilities, which consist primarily of cash and cash equivalents, receivables, and accounts payable and accrued expenses approximate their fair value at <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="29591671" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> based upon the short-term nature of the assets and liabilities. Based on borrowing rates currently available to the Company for loans with similar terms, and the remaining short-term period outstanding, the carrying value of notes payable other than PIK notes approximate fair value. The estimated fair value of the PIK Notes Payable was approximately <em data-td-fact-id="29591672" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$11,395,208</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591673" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$23,361,553</em> at <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="29591674" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> (Level <em data-td-fact-id="29591675" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em>), respectively.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">For the Company&#39;s warrant and PIK note derivative liabilities, Level <em data-td-fact-id="29591676" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em> fair value hierarchy was estimated using a Monte Carlo Model using the following assumptions:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12700" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980457834" id="thunderdome-statement-858307">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591701" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; CONVERTIBLE DEBT (PIK NOTES)</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The Company raised <em data-td-fact-id="29591702" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$23</em> million of financing through the issuance of <em data-td-fact-id="29591703" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> series of Paid-In-Kind (&ldquo;PIK&rdquo;)-Election Convertible Notes in <em data-td-fact-id="29591704" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> (&ldquo;Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591705" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes&rdquo;) and <em data-td-fact-id="29591706" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> (&ldquo;Series A Notes&rdquo;). The original terms of the Series A Notes included among other things: (i) a maturity of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 1, 2018 </em>with an option to extend to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 1, 2019, (</em>ii) a stated interest rate of <em data-td-fact-id="29591707" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10%</em> paid semi-annually and (iii) a conversion price of <em data-td-fact-id="29591708" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.90,</em> adjusted downward based on an anti-dilution provision. The original terms of the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591709" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes included among other things: (i) a maturity of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> August 1, 2023, (</em>ii) a stated interest rate of <em data-td-fact-id="29591710" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10%</em> paid semi-annually and (iii) a conversion price of <em data-td-fact-id="29591711" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.40,</em> adjusted downward based on an anti-dilution provision. On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 14, 2017, </em>an amendment agreement, entered into between the Company and the holders of the Series A Notes and Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591712" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes, went into effect. The agreement resulted in changes to certain terms of the Series A and Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591713" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes. The key terms of the Series A and Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591714" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes, as amended, are highlighted in the table below:<b>&nbsp;</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>

Convertible into shares of the Company<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s common stock at the discretion of the holder or by the Company based on the market price of the Company&rsquo;s stock;</font></p>
			</td>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><u>Series A Notes (Amended)</u></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 3, 2014 (</em>&ldquo;Issue Date&rdquo;), the Company issued, in a private placement pursuant to investment agreements, <em data-td-fact-id="29591718" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$19,848,486</em> principal amount of <em data-td-fact-id="29591719" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10%</em> PIK-Election Convertible Notes due <em data-td-fact-id="29591720" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> (&quot;Series A Notes&quot;) in exchange for <em data-td-fact-id="29591721" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$12,500,000</em> in cash and the cancellation of previously-issued warrants held by <em data-td-fact-id="29591722" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> investor.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The original terms of the Series A Notes included among other things: (i) a maturity of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 1, 2018 </em>with an option to extend to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 1, 2019, (</em>ii) a stated interest rate of <em data-td-fact-id="29591723" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10%</em> paid semi-annually and (iii) a conversion price of <em data-td-fact-id="29591724" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.90,</em> adjusted downward based on an anti-dilution provision. The original terms of both the Series A notes and Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591725" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes can be as exhibits to Forms <em data-td-fact-id="29591726" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8</em>-K filed on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 5, 2014.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Below are key amended terms of the Series A Notes:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

			</td>
		</tr>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width: 100%; text-indent: 0px;">

			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Liquidated Damages</u><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">: The Company is required to pay the noteholders <em data-td-fact-id="29591731" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1%</em> of the principal amount of the Series A Notes if a Registration statement is <em data-td-fact-id="29591732" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> filed and effective within <em data-td-fact-id="29591733" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">90</em> days of the inception date (and further damages for every <em data-td-fact-id="29591734" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">30</em> days thereafter).</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

		<tr style="vertical-align: top;">
			<td style="width: 18pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td style="width: 18pt;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td>
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The number of shares issuable under the Notes <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>be affected by the anti-dilution provisions of the Notes. The antidilition provisions adjust the Exercise Price of the Notes in the event of stock dividends and splits, issuance below the market price of the common stock, issuances below the conversion price of the Notes, pro rata distribution of assets, rights plans, tender offers, and exchange offers.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The entire principal amount of the Series A Notes and accrued interest thereon shall be mandatorily converted into shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock if (i) the Volume Weighted Average Price (&ldquo;VWAP&rdquo;) of the <em data-td-fact-id="29591735" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">thirty</em> (<em data-td-fact-id="29591736" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">30</em>) preceding trading days is at or greater than <em data-td-fact-id="29591737" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.00</em> or the VWAP of the <em data-td-fact-id="29591738" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">ten</em> (<em data-td-fact-id="29591739" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10</em>) preceding trading days is at or greater than <em data-td-fact-id="29591740" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.40;</em> (ii) the closing market price of the shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock is at or greater than <em data-td-fact-id="29591741" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.00;</em> (iii) all outstanding amounts under each Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591742" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Note or replacement financing, if any, shall have been converted into shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock pursuant to the terms of such Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591743" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Note or the replacement financing, if any, on or prior to the date on which a notice of mandatory conversion is received; and (iv) either (<em data-td-fact-id="29591744" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">x</em>) a registration statement is effective and available for the resale of all of the shares into which the Series A Notes convert on the date on which the Series A notes are mandatorily converted and each of the <em data-td-fact-id="29591745" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> (<em data-td-fact-id="29591746" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em>) trading days prior to the date of mandatory conversion and on the date of mandatory conversion the holders of the Series A Notes are <em data-td-fact-id="29591747" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> restricted from selling or distributing any shares into which the Series A Notes convert pursuant to the provisions of the Registration Rights Agreement or (y) the holders Series A Notes <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>sell all such shares into which the Series A Notes convert immediately under Rule <em data-td-fact-id="29591748" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">144</em> under the Securities Act.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">These Series A Notes were <em data-td-fact-id="29591749" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> issued with the intent of effectively hedging any future cash flow, fair value of any asset, liability or any net investment in a foreign operation. In addition to the customary anti-dilution provisions the notes contain a down-round provision whereby the conversion price would be adjusted downward in the event that additional shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock or securities exercisable, convertible or exchangeable for the Company&rsquo;s common stock were issued for cash consideration (e.g. a capital raise) at a price less than the conversion price. Therefore, the estimated fair value of the conversion feature of <em data-td-fact-id="29591750" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9,212,285</em> (based on observable inputs using a Monte Carlo model) was bifurcated from the Series A Notes and accounted for as a separate derivative liability, which resulted in a corresponding amount of debt discount on the Series A Notes. In addition, an additional debt discount of <em data-td-fact-id="29591751" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$7,348,486</em> was recorded as a result of the difference between the <em data-td-fact-id="29591752" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$12,500,000</em> of cash received and the <em data-td-fact-id="29591753" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$19,848,486</em> of principal on the Series A Notes. This combined debt discount of <em data-td-fact-id="29591754" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$16,560,771</em> is being amortized using the effective interest method over the <em data-td-fact-id="29591755" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">9</em>-year term of the Notes as Interest Expense, while the PIK Note Derivative is carried at fair value (using a Monte Carlo model) until the Notes are converted or otherwise extinguished. Any changes in fair value are recognized in earnings.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">36</div>

</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> May 2017 </em>and <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 2017, </em>the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29591756" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,206,289</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591757" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,266,613,</em> respectively, in additional Series A Notes to the holders to pay the semi-annual interest. Additionally, on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 14, 2017, </em>the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29591758" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$324,925</em> of additional Series A Notes, which represented the accrued interest of the Series A Notes on the day on which the terms of the Series A Notes were effectively amended. As part of the amendment agreement, the holders of the Series A Notes received warrants to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591759" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">6,280,000</em> million shares of common stock at <em data-td-fact-id="29591760" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.10</em> per share. The Black Scholes value of these warrants totaled <em data-td-fact-id="29591761" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$298,420</em>.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the fair value of the Series A Note Derivative was estimated to be <em data-td-fact-id="29591762" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,883,630,</em> which includes the value of the derivative related to the additional PIK Notes issued in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> May </em>and <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 2017 </em>for the semi-annual interest payments due and the additional notes issued in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December, 2017. </em>During the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company amortized <em data-td-fact-id="29591763" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5,808,294</em> of debt discount and deferred financing cost relating to the Series A Notes Payable and issued additional PIK Notes in lieu of interest payments of <em data-td-fact-id="29591764" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2,797,836,</em> increasing the Series A Notes Payable carrying value to <em data-td-fact-id="29591765" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$26,909,721</em> as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016, </em>the fair value of the Series A Note Derivative was estimated to be <em data-td-fact-id="29591766" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2,033,643,</em> which includes the value of the derivative related to the additional PIK Notes issued in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> May </em>and <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 2016 </em>for the semi-annual interest payments due. During the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016, </em>the Company amortized <em data-td-fact-id="29591767" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2,549,433</em> of debt discount and deferred financing cost relating to the Series A Notes Payable and issued additional PIK Notes in lieu of interest payments of <em data-td-fact-id="29591768" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2,243,003,</em> increasing the Series A Notes Payable carrying value to <em data-td-fact-id="29591769" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$10,696,047</em> as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><u>Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591770" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes (Amended)</u></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> August 2013, </em>the Company received <em data-td-fact-id="29591771" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$10,500,000</em> of financing through the private placement of <em data-td-fact-id="29591772" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10%</em> mandatory convertible Notes due <em data-td-fact-id="29591773" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> (&quot;Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591774" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes&quot;). The principal amount of the Notes is due on maturity. The Company can elect to pay semi-annual interest on the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591775" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes with additional PIK Notes containing the same terms as the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591776" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes, except interest will accrue from issuance of such notes. The Company can also elect to pay interest in cash. In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> August, 2017, </em>the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29591777" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$703,550</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591778" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$738,728,</em> respectively, in additional Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591779" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes to the holders to pay the semi-annual interest. Additionally, on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 14, 2017, </em>the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29591780" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$577,439</em> of additional <em data-td-fact-id="29591781" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes, which represented the accrued interest of the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591782" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes on the day on which the terms of the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591783" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes were effectively amended.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591784" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes convert into the Company&rsquo;s common stock at a conversion price of <em data-td-fact-id="29591785" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.59</em> per share, which is subject to customary anti-dilution adjustments; the holders <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>convert the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591786" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes at any time. The Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591787" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes are mandatorily convertible after <em data-td-fact-id="29591788" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> year when the weighted average trading price of a share of the common stock for the preceding <em data-td-fact-id="29591789" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">ten</em> trading days is in excess of the conversion price. The Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591790" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes contain customary representations and warranties and several covenants. The proceeds are being used for general corporate purposes. <em data-td-fact-id="29591791" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No</em> broker was used and <em data-td-fact-id="29591792" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> commission was paid in connection with the sale of the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591793" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company was in compliance with the covenants.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">These Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591794" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes were <em data-td-fact-id="29591795" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> issued with the intent of effectively hedging any future cash flow, fair value of any asset, liability or any net investment in a foreign operation. In addition to the customary anti-dilution provisions the notes contain a down-round provision whereby the conversion price would be adjusted downward in the event that additional shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock or securities exercisable, convertible or exchangeable for the Company&rsquo;s common stock were issued for cash consideration (e.g. a capital raise) at a price less than the conversion price. Therefore, the estimated fair value of the conversion feature of <em data-td-fact-id="29591796" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2,055,000</em> (based on observable inputs using a Monte Carlo model) was bifurcated from the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591797" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes and accounted for as a separate derivative liability, which resulted in a corresponding amount of debt discount on the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591798" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes. The debt discount is being amortized using the effective interest method over the <em data-td-fact-id="29591799" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10</em>-year term of the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591800" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes as Interest Expense, while the PIK Note Derivative is carried at fair value (using a Monte Carlo model) until the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591801" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes are converted or otherwise extinguished. Any changes in fair value are recognized in earnings.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the fair value of the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591802" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Note Derivative was estimated to be <em data-td-fact-id="29591803" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$163,634,</em> which includes the value of the derivative related to additional PIK Notes issued in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February </em>and <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> August 2016 </em>for the semi-annual interest payments due and the additional notes issued in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December, 2017. </em>During the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company amortized <em data-td-fact-id="29591804" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$200,360</em> of debt discount and deferred financing cost relating to the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591805" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes Payable and issued additional PIK Notes of <em data-td-fact-id="29591806" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2,019,717</em> in lieu of cash interest payments, increasing the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591807" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes Payable carrying value to <em data-td-fact-id="29591808" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$16,090,721</em> as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017. </em>As part of the amendment agreement, the holders of the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591809" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes received warrants to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591810" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3,720,000</em> million shares of common stock at <em data-td-fact-id="29591811" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.10</em> per share. The Black Scholes value of these warrants totaled <em data-td-fact-id="29591812" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$224,290.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016, </em>the fair value of the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591813" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Note Derivative was estimated to be <em data-td-fact-id="29591814" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$142,909,</em> which includes the value of the derivative related to additional PIK Notes issued in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February </em>and <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> August 2016 </em>for the semi-annual interest payments due. During the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016, </em>the Company amortized <em data-td-fact-id="29591815" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$154,567</em> of debt discount and deferred financing cost relating to the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591816" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes Payable and issued additional PIK Notes in lieu of interest payments of <em data-td-fact-id="29591817" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,308,192,</em> increasing the Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591818" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes Payable carrying value to <em data-td-fact-id="29591819" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$12,344,046</em> as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
