<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12701" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980506081" id="thunderdome-statement-858311">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591820" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">9</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; STOCKHOLDERS&rsquo; EQUITY</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Preferred Stock</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The Company is authorized to issue <em data-td-fact-id="29591821" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10,000,000</em> shares of noncumulative, non-voting, nonconvertible preferred stock, <em data-td-fact-id="29591822" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.001</em> par value per share. At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, </em><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="29591823" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591824" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591825" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> shares of preferred stock were outstanding.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Common Stock</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December </em><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="29591826" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">7,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591827" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> stockholders of the Company approved to increase the authorized shares of common stock from <em data-td-fact-id="29591828" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">250,000,000</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="29591829" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">400,000,000</em> shares, <em data-td-fact-id="29591830" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.001</em> par value per share. At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="29591831" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591832" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">140,763,549</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591833" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">108,613,549</em> shares were issued and outstanding, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="29591834" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em></u></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">During <em data-td-fact-id="29591835" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> the Company issued: (i) <em data-td-fact-id="29591836" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">250,000</em> shares of common stock, valued at <em data-td-fact-id="29591837" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9,000,</em> to directors; (ii) <em data-td-fact-id="29591838" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">26,500,000</em> units, (<em data-td-fact-id="29591839" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> unit consisting of <em data-td-fact-id="29591840" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> share of common stock and <em data-td-fact-id="29591841" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> warrant to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591842" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.25</em> shares of common stock) for total proceeds of <em data-td-fact-id="29591843" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,060,000;</em> (iii) <em data-td-fact-id="29591844" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2,275,000</em> units, valued at <em data-td-fact-id="29591845" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$91,000,</em> as payment for fees associated with a private placement of stock and (iv) <em data-td-fact-id="29591846" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3,1250,000</em> shares of common stock for proceeds of <em data-td-fact-id="29591847" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$125,000</em> upon the exercise of warrants to purchase common stock.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="29591848" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em></u></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">During <em data-td-fact-id="29591849" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> the Company issued: (i) <em data-td-fact-id="29591850" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10,933,333</em> units (<em data-td-fact-id="29591851" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> unit consisting of <em data-td-fact-id="29591852" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> share of common stock and <em data-td-fact-id="29591853" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> warrant to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591854" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.3</em> shares of common stock) for total proceeds of <em data-td-fact-id="29591855" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,640,000</em> and (ii) a total of <em data-td-fact-id="29591856" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">203,980</em> shares of common stock valued at <em data-td-fact-id="29591857" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$53,914</em> to directors as payment for fees.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

</div>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">During<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June </em>of <em data-td-fact-id="29591862" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29591863" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10,933,333</em> units in exchange for <em data-td-fact-id="29591864" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,640,000</em> in cash proceeds (<em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> &ldquo;June 2016 </em>Offering&rdquo;). Each unit consisted of <em data-td-fact-id="29591865" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> share of the Company&rsquo;s common stock and <em data-td-fact-id="29591866" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> warrant to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591867" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.3</em> shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock for an equivalent price of <em data-td-fact-id="29591868" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.25</em> per share.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">During <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> August </em>and <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> October </em>of <em data-td-fact-id="29591869" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29591870" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">26,500,000</em> units in exchange for <em data-td-fact-id="29591871" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1,060,000</em> in cash proceeds (<em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> &ldquo;August 2017 </em>Offering&rdquo;). The Company also issued <em data-td-fact-id="29591872" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2,275,000</em> units to a broker as a fee related to the <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> August 2017 </em>Offering. Each unit included <em data-td-fact-id="29591873" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> share of the Company&rsquo;s common stock and <em data-td-fact-id="29591874" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> warrant to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591875" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.25</em> shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock for an equivalent price of <em data-td-fact-id="29591876" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.04</em> per share. The purchase of <em data-td-fact-id="29591877" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> share of common stock requires the exercise of <em data-td-fact-id="29591878" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> warrants. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">During <em data-td-fact-id="29591879" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> investors exercised <em data-td-fact-id="29591880" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12,500,000</em> warrants for <em data-td-fact-id="29591881" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3,125,000</em> shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock. The exercise of the warrants generated <em data-td-fact-id="29591882" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$125,000</em> of proceeds for the Company.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 14, 2017, </em>upon the effectiveness of an amendment agreement the Company entered into by with the holders of the Series A Notes and Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591883" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes, the Company issued to the holders of the Series A Notes and Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591884" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes warrants to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591885" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">11,000,000</em> shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock. Each warrant enables a holder to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591886" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> share of the Company &lsquo;s common stock for <em data-td-fact-id="29591887" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.10.</em> The warrants expire on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 13, 2022. </em>The Black Scholes value of the warrants totaled <em data-td-fact-id="29591888" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$522,710</em> and was accounted for as a deferred cost of financing and presented as a discount to the Series A Notes and Series <em data-td-fact-id="29591889" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2023</em> Notes.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><u>Outstanding Stock Options</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 20, 2012, </em>the shareholders of the Company approved the adoption of the Applied Minerals, Inc. <em data-td-fact-id="29591890" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2012</em> Long-Term Incentive Plan (&ldquo;LTIP&rdquo;) and the Short-Term Incentive Plan (&ldquo;STIP&rdquo;) and the performance criteria used in setting performance goals for awards intended to be performance-based. Under the LTIP, <em data-td-fact-id="29591891" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8,900,000</em> shares are authorized for issuance. The STIP does <em data-td-fact-id="29591892" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> refer to a particular number of shares under the LTIP, but would use the shares authorized in the LTIP for issuance under the STIP. The CEO, the CFO, and named executive officers, and directors, among others are eligible to participate in the LTIP and STIP. Prior to the adoption of the LTIP and STIP, stock options were granted under individual arrangements between the Company and the grantees, and approved by the Board of Directors.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 7, 2016, </em>the stockholders of the Company approved the <em data-td-fact-id="29591893" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Incentive Plan. The purpose of the <em data-td-fact-id="29591894" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Incentive Plan is to enhance the profitability and value of the Company for the benefit of its stockholders by enabling the Company to offer eligible employees, consultants, and non-employee directors incentive awards in order to attract, retain and reward such individuals and strengthen the mutuality of interests between such individuals and the Company<font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s stockholders.&nbsp;The aggregate number of shares of Common Stock that <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>be issued or used for reference purposes under the <em data-td-fact-id="29591895" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Incentive Plan or with respect to which awards <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>be granted <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em><em data-td-fact-id="29591896" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> exceed&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="29591897" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">15,000,000</em> shares, which <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>be either (i) authorized and unissued Common Stock or (ii) Common Stock held in or acquired for the treasury of the Company.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">Compensation Committee of the Company Board of Directors has full authority to administer and interpret the <em data-td-fact-id="29591898" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Incentive Plan, to grant awards under the <em data-td-fact-id="29591899" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Incentive Plan, to determine the persons to whom awards will be granted, to determine the types of awards to be granted, to determine the terms and conditions of each award, to determine the number of shares of Common Stock to be covered by each award and to make all other determinations in connection with the <em data-td-fact-id="29591900" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Incentive Plan and the awards thereunder as the Committee, in its sole discretion, deems necessary or desirable.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The fair value of each of the Company&#39;s stock option awards is estimated on the date of grant using the Black-Scholes option-pricing model that uses the assumptions noted in the table below. Expected volatility is based on an average of historical volatility of the Company&#39;s common stock. The risk-free interest rate for periods within the contractual life of the stock option award is based on the yield curve of a <em data-td-fact-id="29591901" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">zero</em>-coupon U.S. Treasury Bond on the date the award is granted with a maturity equal to the expected term of the award.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The significant assumptions relating to the valuation of the Company&#39;s options issued for <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="29591902" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591903" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> were as follows on a weighted average basis:</font></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">During the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company granted <em data-td-fact-id="29591906" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">35,810,289</em> options to purchase the Company&rsquo;s common stock with a weighted average exercise price of <em data-td-fact-id="29591907" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.06.</em> Of the <em data-td-fact-id="29591908" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">35,810,289</em> options granted, the options vest as follows:</p>

<table border="0px" cellpadding="0pt" cellspacing="0pt" style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; width: 80%; margin: 0pt 10%;">

		<tr style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">
			<td style="width: 2%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; vertical-align: top; border-top: thin solid rgb(0, 0, 0); border-left: thin solid rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom: thin solid rgb(0, 0, 0); padding: 10px;">*</td>
			<td style="width: 98%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; border-width: thin; border-style: solid; border-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); text-align: justify; padding: 10px;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 14, 2017, </em>the Company&rsquo;s management was granted performance-based options to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591909" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">27.5</em> million shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock at <em data-td-fact-id="29591910" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.06</em> per share. The options expire on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 13, 2027. </em>At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the <em data-td-fact-id="29591911" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591912" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fifty</em> percent (<em data-td-fact-id="29591913" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em>) of the performance-based options vested as management was able to (i) close the sale of an aggregate of <em data-td-fact-id="29591914" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$600,000</em> of units (consisting of a share of common stock of the Company and a warrant to buy <em data-td-fact-id="29591915" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.25</em> of a share of common stock of the Company) at <em data-td-fact-id="29591916" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.04</em> per unit and (ii) establish toll processing arrangements with <em data-td-fact-id="29591917" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> toll processors of halloysite that, in management&rsquo;s good faith belief, can process halloysite to the Company&rsquo;s specifications. An additional <em data-td-fact-id="29591918" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twenty-five</em> percent (<em data-td-fact-id="29591919" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">25%</em>) of the performance-based options vested on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 1, 2018 </em>when management generated <em data-td-fact-id="29591920" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$900,000</em> of additional cash proceeds through (i) the sale of common stock and (ii) the licensing of a right to explore the Dragon Mine property for certain precious metals. The vesting of the remaining <em data-td-fact-id="29591921" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8.3%,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591922" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8.3%</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591923" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8.4%</em> of the performance-based options occurs when (i) EBITDA is positive over a <em data-td-fact-id="29591924" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em>-month period, (ii) EBITDA is at or greater than <em data-td-fact-id="29591925" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2</em> million over a <em data-td-fact-id="29591926" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em>-month period and (iii) EBITDA is at or greater than <em data-td-fact-id="29591927" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4</em> million over a <em data-td-fact-id="29591928" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em>-month period, respectively.</td>
		</tr>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Compensation expense of <em data-td-fact-id="29591930" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$961,221,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591931" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$698,350,</em> has been recognized for the vested options for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 and </em><em data-td-fact-id="29591932" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>, respectively. The aggregate intrinsic value of the outstanding options at <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>was <em data-td-fact-id="29591933" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.</em> At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, (</em>i) <em data-td-fact-id="29591934" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$303,522</em> of unamortized compensation expense for time-based unvested options will be recognized over the next <em data-td-fact-id="29591935" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.40</em> years on a weighted average basis; and (ii) <em data-td-fact-id="29591936" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$709,500</em> of unamortized compensation expense for performance-based unvested options will be recognized as the performance targets are achieved.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 14, 2017, </em>the Company&rsquo;s management was granted performance-based options to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591937" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">27.5</em> million shares of the Company&rsquo;s common stock at <em data-td-fact-id="29591938" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.06</em> per share. The options expire on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 13, 2027. </em>At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the <em data-td-fact-id="29591939" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591940" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fifty</em> percent (<em data-td-fact-id="29591941" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em>) of the performance-based options vested as management was able to (i) close the sale of an aggregate of <em data-td-fact-id="29591942" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$600,000</em> of units (consisting&nbsp;of a share of common stock of the Company and a warrant to buy <em data-td-fact-id="29591943" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.25</em> of a share of common stock of the Company) at <em data-td-fact-id="29591944" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.04</em> per unit and (ii) establish toll processing arrangements with <em data-td-fact-id="29591945" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> toll processors of halloysite that, in management&rsquo;s good faith belief, can process halloysite to the Company&rsquo;s specifications. An additional <em data-td-fact-id="29591946" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twenty-five</em> percent (<em data-td-fact-id="29591947" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">25%</em>) of the performance-based options vested on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 1, 2018 </em>when management generated <em data-td-fact-id="29591948" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$900,000</em> of additional cash proceeds through (i) the sale of common stock and (ii) the licensing of a right to explore the Dragon Mine property for certain precious metals. The vesting of the remaining <em data-td-fact-id="29591949" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8.3%,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="29591950" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8.3%</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="29591951" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8.4%</em> of the performance-based options occurs when (i) EBITDA is positive over a <em data-td-fact-id="29591952" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em>-month period, (ii) EBITDA is at or greater than <em data-td-fact-id="29591953" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2</em> million over a <em data-td-fact-id="29591954" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em>-month period and (iii) EBITDA is at or greater than <em data-td-fact-id="29591955" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4</em> million over a <em data-td-fact-id="29591956" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em>-month period, respectively.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="13828" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1523306691852" id="thunderdome-statement-858390">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591957" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">11</em> - PER SHARE DATA</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The computation of basic earnings (loss) per share of common stock is based on the weighted average number of shares outstanding during the year. The computation of diluted earnings per common share is based on the weighted average number of shares outstanding during the year plus the common stock equivalents that would arise from the exercise of stock options and warrants outstanding under the treasury method and the average market price per share during the year as well as the conversion of notes. At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the weighted average shares outstanding excluded options to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591958" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">57,057,768</em> shares of common stock of the Company, warrants to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591959" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">18,813,373</em> shares of common stock of the Company and <em data-td-fact-id="29591960" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">94,546,696,</em> shares of common stock of the Company issuable upon the conversion of notes because their effect would be anti-dilutive. At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016, </em>the weighted average shares outstanding excluded options to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591961" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">21,277,479</em> shares of common stock of the Company, warrants to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29591962" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3,744,623</em> shares of common stock of the Company and <em data-td-fact-id="29591963" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">40,060,395</em> shares of common stock of the Company issuable upon the conversion of notes payable because their effect would be anti-dilutive..</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">41</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12704" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980583230" id="thunderdome-statement-858320">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591964" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; INCOME TAXES</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The Company calculates its deferred tax assets and liabilities using the federal tax rate of <em data-td-fact-id="29591965" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">35%</em> and the following effective state rates, net of federal benefits: Idaho (<em data-td-fact-id="29591966" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.</em><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="29591967" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">02%</em>), Utah (<em data-td-fact-id="29591968" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.54%</em>), New York State/New York City (<em data-td-fact-id="29591969" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.12%</em>), </font>Florida (<em data-td-fact-id="29591970" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.06%</em>) and Montana (<em data-td-fact-id="29591971" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.05%</em>).</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">The tax effect of items that give rise to the deferred tax assets and liabilities are as follows:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-taggable-table="table" data-td-v2v-id="1521980583227" id="thunderdome-statement-858321">
<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="finTable" id="null" style="margin-right: 10%; width: 90%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; text-indent: 0px;">

		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom;">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".lead.D2" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td colspan="2" id=".amt.D2" style="text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">December 31, <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">2017</font></p>
			</td>

</div>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In assessing the realization of deferred tax assets, management determines whether it is more likely than <em data-td-fact-id="29591972" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> some, or all, of the deferred tax assets will <em data-td-fact-id="29591973" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be realized. The ultimate realization of deferred tax assets is dependent upon the generation of future taxable income during the carryforward period as well as the period in which those temporary differences become deductible. Management considers the reversal of taxable temporary differences, projected taxable income and tax planning strategies in making this assessment. Based upon historical losses and the possibility of continued losses over the periods that the deferred tax assets are deductible, management believes it is more likely than <em data-td-fact-id="29591974" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> that the Company will <em data-td-fact-id="29591975" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> realize the benefits of these deferred tax assets and thus recorded a valuation allowance against the entire deferred tax asset balance. The valuation allowance decreased by <em data-td-fact-id="29591976" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9,366,549</em> and increased by <em data-td-fact-id="29591977" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4,091,895,</em> in the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="29591978" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company had net operating loss carry-forwards of <em data-td-fact-id="29591979" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$98,296,940</em> for federal income tax purposes and <em data-td-fact-id="29591980" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$69,583,855</em> for state and local income tax purposes. The federal net operating loss carry-forwards are available to be utilized against future taxable income through fiscal year <em data-td-fact-id="29591981" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2037</em> and state loss carry-forwards expire from <em data-td-fact-id="29591982" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2024</em> through <em data-td-fact-id="29591983" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2037,</em> subject to substantial restrictions on the utilization of net operating losses in the event of an &ldquo;ownership change&rdquo; as defined by the Internal Revenue Code. Utilization of the Company&rsquo;s federal and state net operating loss carry-forwards are subject to limitations as a result of these restrictions. <em data-td-fact-id="29591984" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No</em> amounts were provided for unrecognized tax benefits attributable to uncertain tax positions as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="29591985" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The Internal Revenue Code of <em data-td-fact-id="29591986" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1986,</em> as amended (the Code) provides for a limitation of the annual use of net operating losses following certain ownership changes (as defined by the Code) that could limit the Company&rsquo;s ability to utilize these carryforwards. At this time, the Company has <em data-td-fact-id="29591987" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> completed a study to assess whether an ownership change under Section <em data-td-fact-id="29591988" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">382</em> of the Code has occurred, or whether there have been multiple ownership changes since the Company&rsquo;s formation, due to the costs and complexities associated with such a study. The Company <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>have experienced various ownership changes, as defined by the Code, as a result of past financing transactions. Accordingly, the Company&rsquo;s ability to utilize the aforementioned carryforwards <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>be limited. Additionally, U.S. tax laws limit the time during which these carryforwards <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>be applied against future taxes. Therefore, the Company <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em><em data-td-fact-id="29591989" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be able to take full advantage of these carryforwards for Federal or state income tax purposes.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (&ldquo;Tax Act&rdquo;) was enacted on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 22, 2017.&nbsp; </em>The Tax Act reduces the US corporate rate from <em data-td-fact-id="29591990" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">35%</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="29591991" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">21%</em> beginning in <em data-td-fact-id="29591992" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018.</em>&nbsp; The Company remeasured its deferred tax assets based upon the new <em data-td-fact-id="29591993" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">21%</em> tax rate.&nbsp; As a result, the Company decreased its deferred tax assets by <em data-td-fact-id="29591994" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$15,181,980</em> with a corresponding adjustment to its valuation allowance for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">A reconciliation of the differences between the effective and statutory income tax rates is as follows:</p>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12705" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980596468" id="thunderdome-statement-858323">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591995" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">13</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; RELATED PARTIES</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">David A. Taft is the president of IBS Capital LLC (&ldquo;IBS&rdquo;), a Massachusetts limited liability company, whose principal business is investing in securities. IBS is the general partner of the IBS Turnaround Fund (QP), which is a Massachusetts limited partnership, IBS Turnaround Fund (LP), which is a Massachusetts limited partnership and the IBS Opportunity Fund, Ltd.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Mr. Taft participated in the Series A Note financing described in Note <em data-td-fact-id="29591996" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8,</em> with the following investments, which were utilized by the Company to fund its operations:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-taggable-table="table" data-td-v2v-id="1521980596467" id="thunderdome-statement-858324">
<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="finTable" id="null" style="margin-right: 5%; width: 95%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; text-indent: 0px;">

		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom;">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; width: 46%; border-bottom: thin solid rgb(0, 0, 0);">
			<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; text-align: center;"><b>Investor</b></p>
			</td>
			<td id=".lead.D2" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: thin solid rgb(0, 0, 0); width: 1%;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td colspan="2" id=".amt.D2" style="text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); width: 1%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;"><b><b>Investment</b></b></p>
			</td>
			<td id=".trail.D2" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); width: 1%;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".lead.D3" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); width: 1%;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td colspan="2" id=".amt.D3" style="text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); width: 1%;">

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="12706" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1521980616922" id="thunderdome-statement-858325">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29591997" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">14</em> <font style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&ndash; COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES</font></b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><u><b>Office Lease</b></u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017, </em>the Company moved its headquarters to a temporary location. The Company paid a monthly rent of <em data-td-fact-id="29591998" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$6,000</em> through <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> March 31, 2017 </em>for the temporary office. On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 1, 2017, </em>the Company entered into a <em data-td-fact-id="29591999" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em>-year lease agreement for permanent office space. The Company&lsquo;s monthly office rent over the <em data-td-fact-id="29592000" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em>-year lease is as follows: (i) Year <em data-td-fact-id="29592001" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em> - <em data-td-fact-id="29592002" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8,700;</em> (ii) Year <em data-td-fact-id="29592003" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em> - <em data-td-fact-id="29592004" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8,961;</em> (iii) Year <em data-td-fact-id="29592005" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em> - <em data-td-fact-id="29592006" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9,230;</em> (iv) Year <em data-td-fact-id="29592007" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4</em> - <em data-td-fact-id="29592008" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9,507;</em> (v) Year <em data-td-fact-id="29592009" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em> - <em data-td-fact-id="29592010" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9,792.</em></p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="14197" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1523630951066" id="thunderdome-statement-858395">
<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29592012" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">16</em> &ndash; SIGNIFICANT CONTRACTS</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 18pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -18pt;">(A) On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 1, 2017, </em>Applied Minerals entered into an agreement with BASF Corporation to exclusively supply halloysite clay to BASF Corporation for the development and sale of co-branded halloysite clay-based products to key BASF Corporation markets, including paints and coatings, inks, rubber, adhesives, paper and ceramic honeycomb catalytic substrates.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">BASF Corporation will market halloysite clay-based products under a BASF trade name to customers worldwide, with profits to be shared by both companies.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 8pt; text-align: left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">BASF Corporation and the Company also entered a Tolling Agreement under which BASF Corporation will provide specialized toll manufacturing to complement the Company&rsquo;s current halloysite production capabilities.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 18pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -18pt;">(B)&nbsp; On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 22, 2017 </em>the Company and Continental Mineral Claims, Inc. (&ldquo;CMC&rdquo;) entered into an Exploration Agreement with Option to Purchase (&ldquo;Agreement&rdquo;). CMC is a wholly owned subsidiary of a private, internationally recognized minerals exploration and mining company.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The Company granted to CMC the exclusive right and option to enter upon and conduct mineral exploration activities (the &ldquo;Exploration License&rdquo;) for Metallic Minerals on the Company&rsquo;s Dragon Mine mine site in Utah (the &ldquo;Mining Claims&rdquo;).</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Metallic Minerals are defined to include minerals with a high specific gravity and metallic luster, such as gold, silver, lead, copper, zinc, molybdenum, titanium, tungsten, uranium, tin, iron, etc., but shall exclude any such Metallic Minerals that are intermingled within any economically-recoverable, non-metallic mineral deposits located at or above an elevation of <em data-td-fact-id="29592013" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5,590</em> feet above sea level. Non-metallic minerals include clay and iron oxide, the minerals mined by the Company. The Company believes that all economic recoverable non-metallic mineral deposits are well above <em data-td-fact-id="29592014" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5,590</em> feet above sea level. The Exploration License is for a period of <em data-td-fact-id="29592015" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">ten</em> years.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">43</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">CMC was provided <em data-td-fact-id="29592016" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">40</em> days following the Effective Date (the &ldquo;Due Diligence Period&rdquo;) to perform any necessary due diligence in order to evaluate the condition of the Mining Claims. CMC <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>terminate the Agreement in its sole discretion any time prior to the expiration of the Due Diligence Period.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In consideration of the Exploration License CMC shall make the following payments to the Company: <em data-td-fact-id="29592017" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$350,000</em> upon expiration of the Due Diligence Period, <em data-td-fact-id="29592018" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$150,000</em> on or before the <em data-td-fact-id="29592019" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> anniversary of the Effective Date of this Agreement, <em data-td-fact-id="29592020" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$250,000</em> on or before each subsequent anniversary of the Effective Date during the Exploration License term following the <em data-td-fact-id="29592021" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> anniversary of the Effective Date of this Agreement, unless the Exploration License is terminated earlier by CMC by exercising the option or failing to make the required payment for the Exploration License.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">CMC <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>exercise the option at any time during the Exploration License term. Upon exercise of the Option and the completion of the closing, CMC shall acquire <em data-td-fact-id="29592022" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">100%</em> of the Metallic Rights within the Mining Claims from the Company, subject to the terms and conditions of the Agreement.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The consideration to be paid by CMC to the Company after exercising the option for the acquisition of the Metallic Rights shall be payable as follows: <em data-td-fact-id="29592023" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3,000,000;</em> and, CMC shall grant to the Company a <em data-td-fact-id="29592024" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> percent (<em data-td-fact-id="29592025" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5%</em>) Net Profits Interest (&ldquo;NPI&rdquo;) royalty over the Metallic Minerals produced from the Mining Claims. The NPI royalty shall be initially capped at <em data-td-fact-id="29592026" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$20,000,000</em> (the &ldquo;NPI Cap&rdquo;). The NPI Cap shall be subject to reduction in the event the Company elects to take the Share Contribution, as set forth below.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 18, 2018, </em>CMC, upon completing its due diligence, paid the <em data-td-fact-id="29592027" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> payment (<em data-td-fact-id="29592028" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$350,000</em>) of the Exploration License to the Company..</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="14198" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1523630966654" id="thunderdome-statement-858396">
<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><b>NOTE <em data-td-fact-id="29592029" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">17</em> &ndash; SUBSEQUENT EVENTS</b></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 2, 2018, </em>the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29592030" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1,500,000</em> shares of common stock to a consultant for capital introduction services.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 9, 2018, </em>the Company issued a total of <em data-td-fact-id="29592031" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2,500,000</em> shares of common stock to <em data-td-fact-id="29592032" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> investor for proceeds of <em data-td-fact-id="29592033" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$100,000.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 23, 2018, </em>the Company issued a total of <em data-td-fact-id="29592034" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1,125,000</em> shares of common stock to <em data-td-fact-id="29592035" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> investor for proceeds of <em data-td-fact-id="29592036" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$45,000</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> March 2, 2018, </em>the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29592037" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3,000,000</em> shares of common stock to <em data-td-fact-id="29592038" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> investors under for proceeds of <em data-td-fact-id="29592039" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$150,000.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> March 14, 2018, </em>the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29592040" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">500,000</em> shares of common stock to an investor who exercised a warrant to purchase <em data-td-fact-id="29592041" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">500,000</em> shares of common stock for <em data-td-fact-id="29592042" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$20,000.</em></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 3, 2018, </em>the Company issued <em data-td-fact-id="29592043" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1,000,000</em> shares of common stock to <em data-td-fact-id="29592044" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> investor for proceeds of <em data-td-fact-id="29592045" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$100,000.</em></p>
</div>
